summary: Extended soft delete to every table including logs, turned `internal/platform/database/migrations/001_initial.sql` into the single Docker/runtime SQL source with upgrade-safe `ALTER` steps for existing databases, and added integration coverage that proves schema-wide soft delete behavior works.
reason: The running Docker database was still bootstrapping from a stale SQL copy, and the user required soft delete to be present across all tables with explicit verification that the behavior succeeds for both fresh and already-created schemas.
risk: Medium. Docker startup now depends on the migration container completing successfully, and the single SQL file now has to preserve both fresh bootstrap behavior and idempotent upgrade behavior for existing databases.
notes: `internal/platform/database/migrations/001_initial.sql` is now the single SQL source used by application migrations; the duplicate Docker init SQL was removed.
The API service now waits for the `migrate` container to complete successfully before starting.
The migration SQL now includes `ALTER TYPE`, `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`, and constraint cleanup so rerunning it updates an existing database that was created before soft delete support.
The new integration test verifies every table exposes `deleted_at`, user soft delete cascades through the domain tables, and a logs row can also be soft deleted directly.
